title: Welcome source validation in CLI: only accept Welcome from current MLS admin (online[0]) — prevents group hijacking
description: cli/src/commands.rs WHISPER_DELIVER handler: binary payload (non-JSON) is treated as MLS Welcome only if the sender matches online[0] (the current MLS admin). This guard prevents a malicious peer from sending a crafted Welcome that pulls the target into an attacker-controlled group. The relay authenticates the sender pub_id (Ed25519 signature), so this is a meaningful check. A contributor might 'simplify' this by removing the admin check, arguing that MLS cryptography protects against bad Welcomes — but an attacker who controls a relay connection can forge a valid Welcome for a group they created, which would pass MLS validation but represent a different group context.
